history.content:
<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The cell to my right at row 07 column E is free floor, while the left side is a wall. I should turn right to face the safe open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing right. The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. Looking at the map, the cells above my current position form a safe vertical corridor, while below and to the left include lava. To align with the safe corridor, I should turn left to face upward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward again at row 07 column D. The cell directly ahead at row 06 column D is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. The left side is blocked by a wall, while the right side at row 07 column E is open floor. Turning right is the safest move to continue along the open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The view matches the earlier layout where I am at the bottom-center with a wall directly in front at row 07 column E, so moving forward is blocked. The open corridor is above me through column D, while the left side is obstructed by lava in nearby cells. The best next step is to turn left to face the safe upward path.
</thought>
<action>
